scope: Phase-1 failure-mode sweep across the assembled engine — transaction atomicity, PrimitiveHost buffer-replay, GrantBackedPolicy commit-time read path, ChangeEvent widening, nested-transaction refusal, system-zone guard, concurrent-writer behavior, large-blob memory posture. All G3 mini-review critical / major dispositions re-verified.
summary: The assembled system holds under controlled failure injection. The four critical/major fixes that closed G3 pass-1 (system-zone guard on inherent put_node + put_edge, RefCell→Mutex panic-safety, ChangeEvent labels carry through deletes) are present, documented, and backed by regression tests. The PrimitiveHost buffer+replay path introduced in G7 pass-2 does NOT introduce new torn-state hazards — the evaluator has no side-effect primitives in Phase 1 (EMIT is a no-op), so a failed replay transaction leaves the graph unchanged and the only observable is an error returned to the caller. GrantBackedPolicy.check_write's grant lookup reads through the engine's Arc<RedbBackend> OUTSIDE the active WriteTransaction — this IS a TOCTOU window (a revocation committed between grant-scan and commit is missed), but it is semantically acceptable for Phase-1 per compromise #2 (Option A) + the declared-AND-checked R1 SC4 contract, and the grant/revocation writes themselves are already engine-privileged so a user subgraph cannot race the policy. Nested transaction still refuses with E_NESTED_TRANSACTION_NOT_SUPPORTED (TxGuard RAII intact; closure side-effects before rejection are impossible because rejection is eager). System-zone guard fires on ALL four write paths (Transaction::put_node/put_edge, RedbBackend::put_node/put_edge). Concurrent 16-thread writer bench remains present and runs under redb's single-writer serialization. The 100MB multi-MB bench is still memory-unguarded (g3-ce-14 style concern, minor, informational-bench scope). Five new minor findings below; zero criticals, zero majors. All G3 deferred items (g3-ce-5/-7/-8/-9/-10/-11) retain their TODO(R4b) / TODO(phase-2) markers and documentation.

g3-ce-1.evidence: crates/benten-graph/src/redb_backend.rs:434-442 — RedbBackend::put_node entry calls guard_system_zone_node(node, is_privileged=false) before put_node_unchecked. put_node_unchecked is fn-private (line 448). NodeStore::put_node trait delegate at line 1085 routes through the guarded entry. guard_system_zone_node at line 51 is the single source of truth.

g3-ce-2.evidence: crates/benten-graph/src/redb_backend.rs:582-597 — RedbBackend::put_edge calls guard_system_zone_edge(edge, false). put_edge_unchecked is fn-private (line 610). Transaction::put_edge at transaction.rs:333-338 applies the same prefix check in-txn.

g3-ce-3.evidence: crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs:1415-1481 — Engine::transaction uses std::sync::Mutex (not RefCell) for ops_cell and user_result. All four lock sites use unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) so PoisonError is recoverable. engine_transaction_policy_panic_safe.rs pins two scenarios (closure-panic, policy-panic).

g3-ce-4.evidence: crates/benten-graph/src/transaction.rs:99-122 — PendingOp::DeleteNode carries labels: Vec<String> AND node: Option<Node>; DeleteEdge carries label + source + target as Options. Read-before-delete happens inside the SAME redb WriteTransaction (transaction.rs:380-397 and 438-458) so the capture is TOCTOU-safe under redb's serializable isolation. to_change_event (line 167-196) maps labels through to ChangeEvent.labels.

g3-ce-5.evidence: TODO(R4b) at transaction.rs:46, :505. Stderr breadcrumb at fan_out (transaction.rs:525-529) still the operator-visible signal floor. Phase-2 will swap in tracing::warn! + dead-letter counter. Not a Phase-1 blocker; no regression.

g3-ce-7.evidence: TODO(phase-2) at redb_backend.rs:197. Same-Arc invariant documented; cross-Arc falls through to redb's single-writer lock (blocks, does not deadlock).

g3-ce-8.evidence: TODO(phase-2) at redb_backend.rs:209. tx_id monotonicity within process; IVM persistence rebuilds-from-scratch on reopen keeps the gap invisible in Phase 1.

g3-ce-9.evidence: concurrent_reader_writer_soak.rs + mvcc_snapshot.rs present. Explicit mid-transaction reader-sees-pre-commit test still not landed; redb's serializable isolation provides the contract and no regression observed.

g3-ce-10.evidence: Ordering contract documented at redb_backend.rs:942-950. IVM author guidance (register-first-then-snapshot) carried.

g3-ce-11.evidence: engine_transaction_policy_panic_safe.rs now covers policy-panic. Subscriber-panic isolation is pinned by benten-ivm/tests/subscriber_panic_isolation.rs + benten-engine/src/change.rs catch_unwind wrap. Real commit-time I/O failure injection remains unaddressed (expensive harness; acceptable Phase-1 posture).

transaction_atomicity.closure_panic: pass — TxGuard Drop releases tx_flag; redb WriteTransaction drops without commit; panic propagates; subsequent .transaction() on SAME Engine works (engine_transaction_policy_panic_safe.rs::closure_panic reuses the same engine).
transaction_atomicity.closure_err: pass — user_result Mutex stores the Err; backend.transaction returns TxAborted; saved result replaces the TxAborted on return. Pinned by failure_injection_rollback.rs.
transaction_atomicity.policy_panic: pass — Mutex unwrap_or_else recovers from poison. Second engine call on the SAME engine was NOT pinned (finding g3-p2-ce-2 pass-2 still open) but the code-side contract is sound.
transaction_atomicity.subscriber_panic: pass — two layers: benten-graph fan_out catches (transaction.rs:521), benten-engine ChangeBroadcast catches (change.rs:122), benten-ivm apply_event catches (subscriber.rs:199). One misbehaving subscriber cannot poison the commit thread nor the other subscribers.
transaction_atomicity.commit_io_failure: untested — no filesystem chaos harness. Accepted as a Phase-1 gap.

primitive_host_buffer_replay.evaluator_success_replay_policy_denial: pass — pending ops drained into replay transaction; check_write fires at commit; replay returns EngineError::Cap; lib.rs:934-939 maps to Outcome{edge:ON_DENIED, error_code}. No partial state visible (replay was the sole commit path).
primitive_host_buffer_replay.evaluator_success_replay_system_zone_rej: pass — lib.rs:940-946 maps GraphError::SystemZoneWrite to Outcome{ON_ERROR, E_SYSTEM_ZONE_WRITE}. User subgraphs cannot smuggle system-labeled nodes through the buffered path because the guard fires inside tx.put_node (transaction.rs:275-283) which is hit during replay.
primitive_host_buffer_replay.evaluator_err_after_buffering: pass — pending_ops are buffered on the ActiveCall frame but the replay transaction is only opened on eval Ok. An eval Err short-circuits before replay; the buffer is drained on frame pop (lib.rs:822-824) regardless. No state lands.
primitive_host_buffer_replay.evaluator_panic_mid_walk: acceptable — the Mutex lock holding active_call is released by Drop (std::sync::Mutex is panic-unaware by design; poison is recoverable via unwrap_or_else). The ActiveCall stack frame leaks (not popped) on panic, but that only matters if the same Engine re-enters dispatch_call — which on panic unwinds through the caller too. No partial backend state possible because the replay transaction never opens.
primitive_host_buffer_replay.intra_op_cid_divergence: pass — projected_cid computed via node.cid() in PrimitiveHost::put_node; replay transaction recomputes via tx.put_node -> node.cid(). Content-addressed hash is deterministic across both sites (benten-core's cid() is pure). No risk of divergence.

grant_backed_policy.grant_lookup_outside_transaction: acceptable-by-compromise — BackendGrantReader::has_unrevoked_grant_for_scope (lib.rs:1905-1949) calls self.backend.get_by_label + get_node, NOT self.backend.snapshot or a read inside the active WriteTransaction. Window: between grant scan and tx commit, a second writer could commit a CapabilityRevocation for the same scope. BUT: (a) revocation writes are engine-privileged (system-zone guard blocks user paths), (b) the compromise #2 Option A contract explicitly accepts 'revocation at iteration N commits at iteration N+1' semantics, (c) the TOCTOU window is bounded by redb's serializable isolation on the grant scan itself — a concurrent grant write lands either fully before or fully after, never partially.
grant_backed_policy.policy_panic: pass — Engine::transaction's Mutex pattern (lib.rs:1415) catches poison on recovery path.
grant_backed_policy.grant_lookup_backend_error: pass — has_unrevoked_grant_for_scope wraps backend errors as CapError::Denied (lib.rs:1880-1883, 1914-1916). Fail-closed: if the policy cannot read grants, the commit is denied. This is the correct posture.

change_event_widening.node_clone_during_emission: pass — ChangeEvent.node = Some(node.clone()) happens inside to_change_event (transaction.rs:148), called on the commit thread AFTER redb commit succeeds. A panic during clone would trigger the catch_unwind at fan_out (transaction.rs:521) and fire the stderr breadcrumb; the transaction stays committed. However Node::clone cannot realistically panic unless the allocator is OOM, in which case the whole process aborts anyway.
change_event_widening.large_node_cloned_per_subscriber: minor concern — fan_out clones the event per (subscriber, op) tuple. With N subscribers + M ops + large node bodies (e.g., 1MB), throughput scales as O(N*M*payload). Phase-1 has ≤ 1 subscriber (IVM) so no observable hit; flagged for Phase 2 when multi-subscriber IVM lands. See r6-ce-4 below.

nested_transaction.same_engine_reentry: pass — TxGuard::try_acquire sees tx_flag=true and returns NestedTransactionNotSupported fast (transaction.rs:210-220). No redb lock touched.
nested_transaction.closure_with_side_effect_before_rejectio: pass — Transaction::transaction (the inner) at transaction.rs:484-489 rejects eagerly without invoking the closure. No g3-ce-12 regression.

system_zone_guard_coverage.NodeStore trait delegate: routes to inherent put_node, therefore guarded
system_zone_guard_coverage.verdict: no remaining bypass — guard is uniform across all six user-reachable write paths

concurrent_writers.16_thread_bench_present: pass — crates/benten-graph/benches/concurrent_writers.rs exists; crates/benten-graph/tests/concurrent_reader_writer_soak.rs is the correctness-level test. Both exercise redb's single-writer serialization. No deadlock, no data race observed in the test suite.
concurrent_writers.nested_arc_different_handles_same_file: documented-acceptable — TODO(phase-2) at redb_backend.rs:197 names the per-Arc scope. Cross-Arc calls serialize via redb's write lock (block, not deadlock).

multi_mb_blobs.100MB_bench_memory_pressure: unchanged from G3 — multi_mb_roundtrip.rs still unguarded by env flag or feature. ~300MB transient heap on the 100MB variant. On a <2GB CI runner this OOM-kills opaquely. Per R4b disposition this is acceptable for an informational bench; flagged as a minor recurrence below (r6-ce-5). Default test suite does NOT run benches, so CI is not affected; only developers explicitly running `cargo bench` hit the memory wall.

findings.claim: Grant-backed policy's has_unrevoked_grant_for_scope reads through Arc<RedbBackend> rather than through a snapshot handle or the active WriteTransaction, creating a TOCTOU window between grant-scan and commit
findings.detail: The policy calls self.backend.get_by_label("system:CapabilityRevocation") + get_by_label("system:CapabilityGrant") + per-CID get_node on each hit. Each of these opens its OWN redb read-txn. Across the scan sequence a concurrent engine-privileged writer could commit a CapabilityRevocation whose scope matches the grant being validated. Window exploitability is bounded because (a) only engine-privileged paths can write system:-prefixed nodes (system-zone guard prevents user paths), (b) Compromise #2 Option A explicitly documents the N+1-iteration delay semantics, (c) the grant lookup sequence is itself linearizable under redb's serializable isolation — partial reads are impossible. Not a Phase-1 blocker; semantic contract is declared-and-honored. Phase-3 identity surface will tighten once actor threading lands.
findings.suggested_fix: No action required for R6. Phase-2 follow-up: swap BackendGrantReader to take a snapshot at the entry of check_write (via backend.snapshot()) OR thread an ActiveTxn handle into CapabilityPolicy::check_write so the grant read joins the committing txn's read-view. Document the current semantics in the GrantBackedPolicy module docstring (it is already mentioned in SECURITY-POSTURE.md compromise #2; add a forward-pointer from grant_backed.rs to that section).

findings.claim: ActiveCall frame leaks on panic — the pop at line 822-824 is not RAII-guarded, so an evaluator panic during run() leaks the frame on the active_call Mutex
findings.detail: dispatch_call pushes a frame (line 809-815), runs dispatch_call_inner (line 818), then pops (line 822-824). If dispatch_call_inner panics (e.g., an allocator OOM, a PrimitiveHost trait-impl panic from a future subgraph), the pop is skipped. The frame sits on the Mutex. Subsequent calls on the SAME engine see the stale frame as the 'current active call' for their PrimitiveHost buffer writes — a second call's put_node would land on the leaked frame's pending_ops (because last_mut() returns the topmost, which is the leaked one). Because the second call ALSO pushes its own frame before running the evaluator (line 809-815), the dispatch_call path is safe: each new call pushes its OWN frame and last_mut returns the correct topmost. The leak is latent — frames accumulate but do not misdirect writes. Over time the Vec grows unbounded, but realistic Phase-1 workloads do not panic mid-dispatch so this is a doc-gap concern, not a correctness issue.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the manual push/pop with an RAII guard: a small struct that pushes on new() and pops on Drop, bound to the `self.active_call` Mutex. Matches the TxGuard pattern in transaction.rs. Two-line change plus one new test that panics mid-dispatch and asserts active_call.lock().len() == 0 afterwards.

findings.claim: emit_event being a Phase-1 no-op is correct for torn-state safety BUT masks a latent G7-style bug where a user subgraph's EMIT primitive fires before the replay transaction commits
findings.detail: A user subgraph can include EMIT primitives. The evaluator invokes PrimitiveHost::emit_event during the walk — which is BEFORE the replay transaction commits (or even opens). If a Phase-2 implementer wires emit_event to the ChangeBroadcast fan-out (the obvious next step for reactive subscribers), EVENT observers will see EMITted events for a dispatch_call that subsequently fails at replay (e.g., policy denial). Torn state. The Phase-1 no-op hides this; a future author may not notice.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a doc-note on PrimitiveHost::emit_event at host.rs:115 and on Engine's impl at lib.rs:2087: 'Phase-2 MUST buffer emits into the ActiveCall frame and replay after successful transaction commit, NOT fire inline during the evaluator walk. Firing inline creates a torn-state hazard when the replay transaction fails.' Single doc-comment, zero runtime cost, prevents a real Phase-2 bug.

findings.claim: fan_out clones the full ChangeEvent (including Option<Node>) per subscriber per op, scaling memory as O(N_subs * M_ops * payload_size)
findings.detail: The loop at line 513-514 iterates subscribers-then-ops-then-to_change_event. Each to_change_event clones the Node inside ChangeEvent.node. With N subscribers and M ops in the batch, a 1MB Node body produces N*M clones of that 1MB. Phase-1 has at most one subscriber (the benten-ivm Subscriber), so N=1 and the hit is invisible. Phase-2 plans multi-subscriber IVM (plus CDC consumers), at which point N=5-10 and the clone cost becomes measurable. Event-payload size is driven by the Node body size the user writes — large blobs (multi-MB payloads per g3-ce-14 neighbor) make it worse.
findings.suggested_fix: Phase-2 refactor: emit Arc<ChangeEvent> rather than ChangeEvent; to_change_event returns Arc<ChangeEvent>; fan_out clones the Arc (cheap) per subscriber. Bench delta at 5 subscribers + 100-op batch + 100KB Node body to confirm the win. Not blocking Phase-1.

findings.claim: 100MB size variant still runs by default with no env-flag or feature-gate guard — OOM-kills opaquely on <2GB RAM runners (g3-ce-14 recurrence)
findings.detail: The module docstring warns (line 29-34) but the size_mb array at line 40-ish still contains the 100MB entry unconditionally. R4b disposition accepted this for informational-bench scope, but the failure mode is opaque — a developer running `cargo bench -p benten-graph` on a laptop with other processes consuming RAM gets a killed process with no message. Does not affect CI because CI does not `cargo bench`.
findings.suggested_fix: Gate the 100MB variant behind `std::env::var("BENTEN_BENCH_LARGE").is_ok()` — three lines, default-off. Developers explicitly opt in when they have the headroom. 1MB + 10MB still runs by default. Matches the posture at R4b.

g3_dispositions_still_valid.g3-ce-11: partially — policy-panic now pinned; commit-time I/O chaos still unpinned (acceptable Phase-1 scope)

untested_scenarios_most_dangerous_phase1: commit-time redb I/O failure (disk full / permission denied) during a user transaction — no injection harness exists. Manual chaos: fill the disk, run a write, observe error surface.
ActiveCall frame leak on mid-dispatch panic (r6-ce-2) — zero test coverage for the panic path of dispatch_call_inner.
GrantBackedPolicy racing with engine-privileged revocation commit (r6-ce-1) — documented as compromise-accepted, but no test exercises the interleaving explicitly.
Multi-subscriber fan_out throughput degradation at 5-10 subscribers (r6-ce-4) — Phase-1 has 1 subscriber so the scaling behavior is unobserved until Phase 2.
notes: Phase-1 pipeline is chaos-resilient within its declared scope. The assembled engine exhibits the three chaos-engineering hallmarks: fail-closed defaults (system-zone guard + policy denial on backend read error), bounded blast radius (per-handler buffer+replay isolates state), and observable panic containment (three nested catch_unwind layers at graph / engine / IVM boundaries). The five minor findings are all doc-and-polish grade; none warrant pre-R6 revision. R6 council verdict from this seat: PASS-WITH-MINORS. File the five items on the R4b/Phase-2 docket alongside the still-open G3 deferred items. Do NOT re-spin Phase 1.
